results.query: Does VoltCare cover accidental damage if I drop my phone?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare accidental damage coverage phone drop physical damage claim
results.suggested_response: Yes, VoltCare covers accidental damage including drops, but there's an excess fee. For phones under £300 it's £25, £300-£799 is £49, and £800+ is £75. The excess is paid before your repair is booked. Coverage depends on your specific VoltCare plan level.
results.notes: Clear policy answer — Plus and Complete cover AD, Essential does not

results.query: How do I make a VoltCare claim?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare warranty claim process procedure how to submit
results.suggested_response: To make a VoltCare claim, please call us on 0333 400 7000 or visit any Voltex store. Our colleagues will help you through the process and explain your options. We can also help you understand whether VoltCare or your statutory rights would be better for your specific situation.
results.notes: Straightforward claim process question

results.query: I bought my VoltCare plan in 2023 — do I still have to pay the excess on accidental damage?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare plan 2023 accidental damage excess fee coverage terms
results.suggested_response: Good news! Since you bought your VoltCare plan in 2023, you're on our Version 4.5 terms which means no excess applies to accidental damage claims on the Plus tier. If you're ever asked to pay an excess, just ask us to verify your plan version and we'll correct any error immediately.
results.notes: Version history question — pre-Oct 2024 plans have no excess. Agent must check CRM.

results.query: My OLED TV has burn-in — is that covered under VoltCare Complete?
results.rewritten_query: OLED TV burn-in screen fault VoltCare Complete warranty coverage claim
results.suggested_response: Unfortunately, OLED burn-in isn't covered under any VoltCare plan, including Complete. This is because burn-in is a known characteristic of OLED display technology rather than a product fault. All manufacturers exclude this from their warranties too. I'd recommend using your TV's built-in pixel refresh feature regularly to help minimize this risk.
results.notes: OLED burn-in is explicitly excluded — common customer misconception

results.query: I have VoltCare on my washing machine and it broke down — how long will the repair take and what if they can't fix it?
results.rewritten_query: VoltCare washing machine repair timeframe turnaround replacement policy unrepairable
results.suggested_response: For VoltCare washing machine repairs, we aim to visit within 3-7 business days. If parts are needed, we source them in 3-5 days. If we can't complete the repair within 15 business days of your claim, we'll discuss replacement options with you.
results.notes: Multi-part question covering white goods SLA and replacement trigger

results.query: My neighbour repaired my laptop before I knew about VoltCare — will the claim still be valid?
results.rewritten_query: unauthorised repair third party voided VoltCare cover laptop warranty claim validity
results.suggested_response: I need to check the specific terms around unauthorised repairs for you. The knowledge base doesn't contain clear information about how third-party repairs affect VoltCare coverage, so I'll need to escalate this to get you the correct answer.
results.escalation_reason: Policy clarification needed on whether unauthorised third-party repairs void VoltCare coverage - not clearly covered in available knowledge base
results.notes: Unauthorised repair voids cover — but agent cannot confirm without physical inspection

results.query: I bought a laptop two weeks ago and it has stopped working — what are my options?
results.rewritten_query: laptop hardware failure repair warranty claim Consumer Rights Act statutory rights
results.suggested_response: Since your laptop developed a fault within 30 days of purchase, you have the right to a full refund under the Consumer Rights Act. You can choose a refund, repair, or replacement - it's entirely your choice. If you also have VoltCare, that's another option available to you.
results.notes: Within 30-day window — statutory right to full refund applies

results.query: I changed my mind about a TV I bought online three days ago — can I return it?
results.rewritten_query: online purchase return cooling off period distance selling regulations Consumer Rights Act 14 day return window
results.suggested_response: Good news! Since you bought online, you have a 14-day statutory right to cancel for any reason. You're well within the timeframe - just let us know you want to cancel within 14 days of delivery, then return it within another 14 days. We'll refund you once we receive it back.
results.notes: Online purchase within 14-day cancellation right under Consumer Contracts Regulations

results.query: My TV arrived damaged — I only noticed after the delivery team left. What happens now?
results.rewritten_query: delivery damage discovered after delivery team departure damaged goods consumer rights returns
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry your TV arrived damaged. This sounds like transit damage, which means you're covered under your statutory rights. I can arrange a return and replacement for you right away. For a TV, we'll need to collect it from your home - I can book this in for you now.
results.notes: Damage discovered post-delivery — 48-hour window applies

results.query: I dropped my laptop and cracked the screen — can I return it for a refund?
results.rewritten_query: laptop accidental damage return refund policy customer damage exclusions
results.suggested_response: I understand that's frustrating. Unfortunately, accidental damage like dropping your laptop isn't covered under our return policy as it's not a product fault. If you have VoltCare Plus or Complete protection, this would be covered as an accidental damage claim. Without VoltCare, we can provide a paid repair quote. Would you like me to check your VoltCare coverage?
results.notes: AD is not a return — redirect to VoltCare AD claim or paid repair

results.query: I bought a TV 8 months ago and the screen has developed coloured lines — is Voltex responsible?
results.rewritten_query: TV screen fault manufacturing defect warranty statutory rights 8 months
results.suggested_response: Yes, we can help with this. At 8 months, this would likely be covered as a manufacturing defect under your statutory rights. The manufacturer warranty may also still apply. Do you have VoltCare protection? I'll need to check your purchase details and arrange the best repair option for you.
results.notes: After 6 months burden shifts to customer — but screen fault at 8 months is likely a defect

results.query: The product listing said this laptop has a dedicated GPU but it only has integrated graphics — I want a full refund
results.rewritten_query: laptop product misdescription GPU integrated graphics not as described statutory rights Consumer Rights Act full refund
results.suggested_response: I understand your concern about the GPU specification not matching the product listing. A product not being as described is covered under the Consumer Rights Act. Since this appears to be a misdescription, you're entitled to a full refund. Let me process this return for you right away.
results.escalation_reason: Potential misdescription — statutory rights apply at any time; escalate to Trading Standards team
results.notes: Misdescription — statutory right at any time, must escalate to Trading Standards team

results.query: What is the difference between OLED and QLED televisions?
results.rewritten_query: OLED versus QLED television technology differences display specifications buying advice
results.suggested_response: OLED TVs have pixels that light themselves and turn completely off for perfect blacks - great for dark rooms and movies. QLED uses enhanced LED backlighting with quantum dots for brighter images - better for well-lit rooms. OLED offers superior contrast, while QLED provides higher brightness. Both are excellent choices depending on your viewing environment.
results.notes: Direct FAQ match — highest similarity expected

results.query: How much RAM do I need in a laptop for everyday use?
results.rewritten_query: laptop RAM memory requirements everyday computing specifications buying advice
results.suggested_response: For everyday use like web browsing, email, and video calls, I'd recommend 16GB RAM. This handles multitasking comfortably and is our current standard. 8GB can feel sluggish with many browser tabs open. Since most modern laptops can't be upgraded later, it's worth investing in 16GB now rather than replacing the laptop sooner.
results.notes: Direct FAQ match

results.query: I want to use my new TV for gaming on PlayStation 5 — what should I look for?
results.rewritten_query: TV specifications gaming PlayStation 5 compatibility HDMI 2.1 4K 120Hz VRR ALLM input lag
results.suggested_response: For PS5 gaming, you'll need a TV with HDMI 2.1 port for 4K 120fps gaming. Look for VRR (Variable Refresh Rate) to reduce screen tearing and ALLM (Auto Low Latency Mode) for automatic game mode switching. Our recommended gaming TVs include LG OLED, Samsung QN90D/QN95D, and Sony Bravia 9 series.
results.notes: Specific gaming TV requirements — HDMI 2.1 is the key spec

results.query: Is a Chromebook suitable for a university student studying engineering?
results.rewritten_query: Chromebook specifications engineering student university requirements software compatibility CAD programming
results.suggested_response: For engineering studies, a Chromebook wouldn't be suitable as engineering requires specialized software like CAD and 3D modelling tools that only run on Windows. Engineering students need a laptop with a dedicated GPU and 16GB+ RAM to handle these demanding applications effectively.
results.notes: Chromebook not suitable for engineering software — important to get right

results.query: What washing machine drum size do I need for a family of four?
results.rewritten_query: washing machine drum capacity size family of four household size buying advice
results.suggested_response: For a family of four, I'd recommend looking at 10kg drum capacity washing machines. These are specifically designed for 3-4 people or families who prefer doing fewer, larger washes. This size handles family loads well while giving you flexibility for bulky items like duvets.
results.notes: Straightforward buying guide question

results.query: My laptop battery drains really fast — is that covered under warranty?
results.rewritten_query: laptop battery degradation premature failure warranty coverage charge cycles
results.suggested_response: Fast battery drain can be covered if it's a sudden failure or happening well before the expected lifespan. Battery degradation over time is normal wear, but premature failure may be covered. We'd need to assess your specific case at our repair centre to determine if it's a covered fault.
results.notes: Battery degradation vs premature failure — nuanced answer needed

results.query: My washing machine was supposed to be delivered today but it hasn't arrived
results.rewritten_query: large item delivery missed failed delivery washing machine
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry your washing machine didn't arrive today as expected. Let me check your delivery details and investigate what's happened. I'll contact our fulfilment team to track your order and arrange the next available delivery slot for you at no extra charge.
results.notes: Missed large item delivery — standard process

results.query: Can I cancel my order before it is delivered?
results.rewritten_query: order cancellation before delivery amendment cancel
results.suggested_response: Yes, you can cancel your order free of charge as long as it hasn't been despatched yet. You can cancel online at voltex.co.uk/my-orders or call us on 0333 400 7000. Once despatched, it becomes a return instead. Would you like me to help you cancel it now?
results.notes: Cancellation before despatch is straightforward

results.query: I need to change my delivery address — the order was placed an hour ago
results.rewritten_query: order delivery address change amendment recent order modification
results.suggested_response: I can help change your delivery address. Since your order was placed recently, this should be possible. I'll need to handle this over the phone as address changes can't be done online. Can you confirm if this is for a small item or large item delivery?
results.notes: Address change requires phone call and updating two systems

results.query: I want Voltex to install my new washing machine when they deliver it
results.rewritten_query: VoltInstall washing machine installation service delivery
results.suggested_response: Absolutely! We offer VoltInstall washing machine installation for £49. This includes disconnecting your old machine (if you have one), connecting the new one to your existing plumbing, and running a test cycle. You can add this at checkout online or by calling 0333 400 7000 before your delivery date.
results.notes: VoltInstall must be added 48 hours before delivery

results.query: I live in the Scottish Highlands — can I get my fridge-freezer delivered and will there be extra charges?
results.rewritten_query: Scottish Highlands delivery coverage large appliance fridge-freezer remote area surcharge
results.suggested_response: Yes, we can deliver your fridge-freezer to the Scottish Highlands! There's a £9.99 surcharge for large items to cover the extra transport costs, which you'll see clearly at checkout. Delivery will take an extra 2-3 business days compared to standard areas.
results.notes: Postcode restriction with surcharge — specific detail required

results.query: The delivery team said they couldn't get my fridge through the door — what happens now?
results.rewritten_query: large item delivery access failure failed delivery doorway width fridge
results.suggested_response: I understand the delivery team couldn't get your fridge through the door. We'll need to rebook a redelivery for you. However, there may be a £25 failed delivery charge if the access wasn't accurately described when you ordered. Let me check your order details and arrange the redelivery.
results.escalation_reason: Access failure on large item delivery — failed delivery charge waiver assessment required
results.notes: Access failure — failed delivery charge applies, but refund available for access issues

results.query: I am going to France next month — will I be charged extra to use my phone?
results.rewritten_query: EU roaming VoltMobile phone usage abroad
results.suggested_response: It depends on your plan! If you have 60GB or above, EU roaming is free - use your allowance as normal. For plans under 60GB, you'll need a £10 EU Roaming Pass for 14 days (10GB data, unlimited calls/texts) or face high charges. What plan are you on?
results.notes: EU roaming — depends on plan tier

results.query: How do I bring my existing phone number to VoltMobile?
results.rewritten_query: number porting PAC code transfer existing phone number VoltMobile
results.suggested_response: To bring your number to VoltMobile, first get a PAC code by texting PAC to 65075 (free) or calling your current network. Then provide this PAC when activating your VoltMobile SIM - online, in-store, or by calling 0333 400 9000. Your number will typically transfer by midnight on the first working day after we receive your PAC.
results.notes: Number porting process — clear PAC code instructions

results.query: I have been charged for roaming but I never left the UK
results.rewritten_query: border proximity roaming charge dispute goodwill credit
results.suggested_response: I understand your concern about the roaming charges. Let me check your account to see the usage records and location data for when these charges occurred. I'll also verify if you received our standard roaming advisory message. This will help us determine what happened and resolve this for you.
results.escalation_reason: Team Leader approval required for any goodwill credits for roaming charge disputes
results.notes: Border proximity roaming — escalate to TL for goodwill credit assessment

results.query: My phone has been stolen — what should I do right now?
results.rewritten_query: phone theft stolen device insurance VoltCare mobile phone protection claim procedure
results.suggested_response: I'm sorry to hear your phone was stolen. Unfortunately, VoltCare doesn't cover theft or loss. For theft protection, you'd need home contents insurance or VoltMobile insurance if you have that. I'd recommend contacting the police first, then your network provider to block the SIM.
results.notes: Lost/stolen — bar SIM immediately, advise police report for insurance

results.query: I want to cancel my 24-month phone plan — I have 14 months left and I also owe money on the handset
results.rewritten_query: contract cancellation early termination VoltMobile SIM plan handset finance outstanding balance
results.suggested_response: I can help you cancel your phone plan. You'll need to pay two separate charges: an early termination fee for the remaining service months, plus the full outstanding balance on your handset. You can check your exact handset balance in the VoltMobile app under 'My Agreement'. Would you like me to calculate your total cancellation cost?
results.notes: Must quote BOTH service ETC and handset settlement — critical agent error if only one quoted

results.query: There is a charge on my bill for a third-party service I did not sign up for
results.rewritten_query: third party service unauthorized billing charge dispute not requested
results.suggested_response: I can help with that third-party charge. Let me check your bill to identify the service provider. I'll apply a third-party charge bar to prevent future charges and raise a dispute on your behalf. You can also contact the third-party directly to cancel and request a refund. The dispute process takes 14-28 business days.
results.notes: Third-party charge — apply bar immediately, raise dispute, possible goodwill credit
